glossary.vix_desc: VIX measures expected volatility in the US stock market. It strongly affects Japanese stocks too. A higher VIX means more fear and uncertainty among market participants.
glossary.vix_safe_desc: Markets are calm. Normal entries are fine
glossary.vix_caution_desc: Somewhat unstable. Keep position sizes smaller
glossary.vix_warning_desc: High volatility. Only low-ATR stocks with minimal exposure
glossary.vix_danger_desc: Panic market. Avoid all new entries
glossary.screening_desc: Screen stocks first, then generate your plan. ATR (volatility) and volume filters keep only stable, tradeable candidates.
glossary.col_code: Stock code — the ticker number on the Tokyo Stock Exchange (e.g. 7203 = Toyota)
glossary.col_day_change: Percentage change from previous day's close. Positive = up, negative = down
glossary.col_avg_vol: Average daily trading volume over the past 20 days (thousands of shares). Higher = more liquid, easier to fill orders
glossary.col_atr14: ATR14 (Average True Range, 14 days) — the average daily price range as a % of current price. Lower = more stable, stop-loss levels work better
glossary.col_ma25: Deviation from the 25-day moving average. Positive (green) = above MA25 (uptrend signal), negative (red) = below MA25
glossary.col_range: Where the current price sits within the past 52-week range. 0% = near 52-week low, 100% = near 52-week high
glossary.term_atr14_desc: The average daily high-to-low range over the past 14 days, expressed as a percentage of the current price. Example: ATR=2% means the stock typically moves about 2% per day. Lower ATR = more predictable moves and more reliable stop-loss levels.
glossary.term_ma25_desc: The difference between the current price and the 25-day average price, as a percentage. Positive = trading above average (uptrend signal). Negative = trading below average. Extreme values may indicate overextension.
glossary.term_range_desc: Where the current price sits between the 52-week low (0%) and high (100%). Mid-range (30–60%) is generally safer. Near 100% means the stock is at yearly highs — chasing can be risky.
glossary.term_vol_desc: Average daily trading volume (in thousands of shares) over the last 20 days. Higher volume = more active trading = easier to fill your limit orders. Low-volume stocks may leave orders unfilled.
glossary.term_day_change_g_desc: Percentage change from the previous close to the current close. +2% means the stock rose 2%, -3% means it fell 3%. Large moves often continue into the next day.
glossary.plan_desc: AI generates tomorrow's limit and stop-loss order prices based on your screened stocks. Review the plan, then set your orders at your broker before market open.
glossary.plan_result_note: AI suggestions — final decisions are yours
glossary.plan_disclaimer: ⚠ This plan is AI-generated information only and does not constitute investment advice. All trading decisions are your own responsibility.
